primary_segment: AI GPUs, NVLink/InfiniBand and CUDA software stack
notes: Figures reused from 01-semiconductor, same filing, not re-derived. Carried from that row: Market cap ~US$5.11T as of 2026-07-12 (companiesmarketcap.com). USD reporter, no FX conversion needed. Dominant AI accelerator designer; CUDA software lock-in makes switching costly even where rival silicon exists; shared node with the semiconductor map.

primary_segment: Instinct GPU accelerators
notes: Figures reused from 01-semiconductor, same filing, not re-derived. Carried from that row: Semiconductor company; figures from FY2025 10-K (filed 2026-02). Market cap ~909.69B mid-July 2026 from stockanalysis.com (secondary; range 882-910B). Main credible alternative to Nvidia accelerators but single-digit-to-low-teens datacenter GPU share.

primary_segment: Gaudi AI accelerators; also a foundry
notes: Figures reused from 01-semiconductor, same filing, not re-derived. Carried from that row: IDM pivoting to contract foundry. Net income figure is a small net loss. Capex is gross additions to property, plant and equipment before government incentives/partner contributions (net capex was lower). Market cap ~US$518B as of 2026-07-13 (stockanalysis.com); other trackers show $460-566B this month, reflecting high volatility. USD reporter, no FX conversion needed. Gaudi/Falcon Shores line has struggled for share; included mainly for foundry overlap with semiconductor map.

primary_segment: Custom AI ASIC co-design (Google TPU, Meta MTIA) and Tomahawk/Jericho switch silicon
notes: Figures reused from 01-semiconductor, same filing, not re-derived. Carried from that row: Semiconductor company; figures from FY2025 10-K. Net income taken from XBRL ProfitLoss tag (Broadcom did not populate NetIncomeLoss annually in this filing; NCI is negligible so it equals net income). Market cap ~1902.89B as of 2026-07-10 from stockanalysis.com (secondary source; sources ranged 1830-2000B that week). De facto sole partner for hyperscaler custom AI ASIC programs plus a merchant-silicon duopoly (with Marvell) in AI cluster switch chips; strong but a second source (Marvell) exists, so not a single point.

primary_segment: Custom AI silicon co-design and optical/electrical interconnect
notes: Second source to Broadcom for custom AI ASIC and interconnect programs; growing but not yet a sole-source chokepoint.

primary_segment: Wafer-scale AI training/inference chips
notes: Niche wafer-scale architecture; filed and withdrew IPO plans; remains private as of mid-2026.

primary_segment: LPU inference-only accelerators
notes: Inference-specialist chip startup; small share of overall AI compute.

primary_segment: High-bandwidth memory (HBM) for AI accelerators
notes: Figures reused from 01-semiconductor, same filing, not re-derived. Carried from that row: Key HBM supplier for AI. Figures per SK hynix's SEC Form F-1 (filed ahead of its Jul 2026 Nasdaq listing), which converts KRW at W1,523.5 = US$1.00 (Mar 31 2026 Fed noon buying rate). Revenue KRW97,146,675mn, net income (profit for year) KRW42,948bn, R&D KRW6,466bn, capex KRW27,519bn. Market cap ~US$1.03T as of 2026-07-10, days after Nasdaq debut (source varies $877B-$1.07T across trackers). Leading HBM supplier to Nvidia; HBM output is capacity-constrained industry-wide; shared node with semiconductor map.

primary_segment: HBM memory, memory chips, foundry and devices
notes: Figures reused from 01-semiconductor (as 'Samsung' there), same filing, not re-derived. Carried from that row: Vertically integrated across four roles. Net income = profit attributable to owners of parent (total incl. non-controlling interests was $31.79B). Converted from KRW at implied rate ~1,421.8 KRW/US$1 per Samsung's own USD-translated statements. Capex = cash purchase of PP&E per official cash flow statement (KRW47.52tn); a separate 'facility investment' figure of KRW52.7tn also circulates in press coverage. Market cap ~US$1.11T as of Jul 2026 (companiesmarketcap.com). One of only three companies that can make HBM at volume; also a foundry/packaging alternative; shared node with semiconductor map.

primary_segment: HBM and DRAM memory for AI servers
notes: Figures reused from 01-semiconductor (as 'Micron' there), same filing, not re-derived. Carried from that row: Memory (DRAM/NAND) semiconductor company. FY2025 10-K filed 2025-10-03. Capex is large relative to revenue due to HBM/DRAM fab investment. Market cap ~1060B as of 2026-07-13 from stockanalysis.com (secondary). Third member of the HBM oligopoly; HBM demand has outstripped supply through 2025-2026.

primary_segment: Leading-edge fabrication and CoWoS advanced packaging for AI accelerators
notes: Figures reused from 01-semiconductor, same filing, not re-derived. Carried from that row: Converted from NT$ at TSMC's own filed weighted-average rate of NT$31.11 = US$1.00 (per 20-F): revenue NT$3,809.05bn, net income NT$1,717.88bn, R&D NT$246,427mn, capex NT$1,272,411mn. Market cap ~US$2.25T as of Jul 2026 (companiesmarketcap.com). Sole leading-edge foundry for Nvidia/AMD/Google/Amazon AI silicon; CoWoS advanced-packaging capacity is a widely reported bottleneck on AI chip supply; shared node with semiconductor map.

primary_segment: High-speed Ethernet switches for AI back-end clusters
notes: Leading AI-cluster networking vendor but competes with Cisco, Juniper and white-box Broadcom-based switches.

primary_segment: PCIe/CXL retimers and connectivity chips linking GPUs, memory and networking
notes: Fast-growing signal-integrity specialist inside AI servers; small but increasingly hard to route around as PCIe generations advance.

primary_segment: AI server and rack-scale system integration
notes: Fast-turnaround AI server builder; competes with Dell, HPE and ODMs.

primary_segment: Enterprise AI servers and infrastructure
notes: Major AI server OEM for enterprise buyers; substitutable by other integrators.

primary_segment: Enterprise AI servers and infrastructure
notes: Major AI server OEM; substitutable.

primary_segment: Contract assembly of AI servers and GPU baseboards
notes: Largest assembler of Nvidia GB-series systems; alternatives exist in Quanta, Wistron, Wiwynn but Foxconn holds outsized share.

primary_segment: Data center power, thermal management and liquid cooling systems
notes: Leading supplier of liquid-cooling and power infrastructure for AI racks; the vendor set (with Schneider, Boyd, others) is capacity-strained but not sole-source, so treated as a theme, not a chokepoint.

primary_segment: Electrical distribution, UPS and cooling for data centers
notes: Major data-center power/cooling vendor; competes directly with Vertiv and Eaton.

primary_segment: Power management, switchgear and large transformers
notes: Power management, switchgear and transformers with multi-year backlogs; the transformer/switchgear bottleneck is real but spread across several makers, so not a single irreplaceable node.

primary_segment: Grid transformers, turbines and electrical grid equipment
notes: One of a handful of manufacturers of large power transformers; global transformer lead times have stretched to years, gating grid interconnection for new data centers.

primary_segment: Cloud infrastructure (AWS) plus custom silicon (Trainium/Inferentia/Graviton)
notes: Figures reused from 01-semiconductor (as 'Amazon' there), same filing, not re-derived. Carried from that row: Whole-company figures; semiconductor is a small part (Graviton/Trainium/Inferentia). Figures from FY2025 10-K (filed 2026-02-06). R&D left blank: Amazon does not disclose a discrete R&D line; R&D is bundled into Technology and infrastructure (108.52B for FY2025, which also includes AWS server/datacenter costs), so a clean R&D figure is not reported. Capex is Purchases of property and equipment. Market cap ~2639B as of 2026-07-14 from stockanalysis.com (secondary). One of three hyperscalers; buyers can and do route workloads between AWS, Azure and Google Cloud, so cloud concentration is a theme (see FOUNDATION.md), not a single point. Shared node with semiconductor map (custom silicon).

primary_segment: Azure cloud infrastructure and exclusive OpenAI compute/commercial partner
notes: Figures reused from 01-semiconductor, same filing, not re-derived. Carried from that row: Whole-company figures; semiconductor is a small part (custom Azure/Maia AI chips). FY2026 ended 2026-06-30 but its 10-K/earnings 8-K were not filed yet as of 2026-07-14, so FY2025 10-K (filed 2025-07-30) is the latest audited year. Market cap ~2917B as of 2026-07-14 from stockanalysis.com (secondary). Controls the primary compute/distribution channel for OpenAI, and among the largest AI capex spenders, but workloads can route to other clouds, so not a single-point chokepoint.

primary_segment: Google Cloud infrastructure, in-house TPUs and Gemini models
notes: Figures reused from 01-semiconductor (as 'Alphabet (Google)' there), same filing, not re-derived. Carried from that row: Whole-company figures; semiconductor is a small part (TPUs/custom silicon embedded in Google Cloud, not broken out). Figures from FY2025 10-K (filed 2026-02). Revenue tagged as Revenues. Market cap ~4300B as of 2026-07-13 from stockanalysis.com (secondary; range 4300-4340B). Only hyperscaler with a mature in-house AI accelerator (TPU) reducing its own Nvidia dependence, and runs a leading model lab (DeepMind/Gemini); still routable-around at the cloud layer, so it is not flagged as a chokepoint.

primary_segment: Self-operated AI datacenter fleet and Llama open-weight models
notes: Figures reused from 01-semiconductor (as 'Meta' there), same filing, not re-derived. Carried from that row: Whole-company figures; semiconductor is a small part (custom AI silicon is a minor fraction; most capex is AI datacenter buildout). Figures from FY2025 10-K (filed 2026-01-29). Market cap ~1698B as of ~2026-07-10 from stockanalysis.com (secondary). Massive AI capex and largest open-weight model family, but does not sell cloud capacity to third parties so is not a routing chokepoint in the same sense as AWS/Azure/GCP.

primary_segment: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) for AI training/inference
notes: Fast-growing AI cloud/back-end capacity provider (notably for OpenAI) but a distant fourth in hyperscale share.

primary_segment: GPU-specialist ('neocloud') cloud infrastructure
notes: Largest independent GPU cloud; concentrated customer base (Microsoft/OpenAI/Nvidia-linked) makes it systemically notable but replaceable at the margin.

primary_segment: GPU-specialist cloud infrastructure (former Yandex cloud unit)
notes: European/emerging neocloud GPU provider; smaller scale alternative to CoreWeave.

primary_segment: Frontier foundation models (GPT series) and ChatGPT
notes: Leading model lab by consumer/enterprise adoption, but Anthropic, Google and open-weight models are substitutes at the model layer; the real chokepoints sit in compute/power beneath it.

primary_segment: Frontier foundation models (Claude family)
notes: Major model lab; substitutable at the model layer relative to physical/compute chokepoints.
